title: pilosella bauhini subsp. albaecclesiensis
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. albaecclesiensis, also known as White Horse's Tail, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Pilosella genus, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. albaecclesiensis include reducing fever, alleviating joint pain, and supporting digestive health, as well as its potential in lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits and support overall well-being.
